Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio
==================================================

.. math::

   \text{}

.. raw:: html

   <style>
      .kpi-table {
         width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; margin-bottom: 1.5em;
         border: 1px solid #e0e0e0; table-layout: fixed;
      }
      .kpi-table td {
         padding: 12px; vertical-align: top; border: 1px solid #e0e0e0;

      }

         width: 25%; font-weight: bold; background-color: #f7f7f7;
      }

   <div style="margin-bottom: 1.5em; display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px; flex-wrap: wrap;">

   </div>

   <table class="kpi-table">
      <tbody>
         <tr>
            <td>Description</td>
            <td><ul><li>The Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio is a financial metric that compares the costs associated with maintenance activities to the total operating costs of a facility</li><li>This ratio provides insight into how much of the overall budget is allocated to maintenance efforts</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Objective</td>
            <td><ul><li>The primary objective of this KPI is to assist in budgeting and to evaluate the overall financial impact of the maintenance strategy on the operational costs</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Formula</td>
            <td>
               <p>This ratio is calculated by dividing the total maintenance costs by the total operating costs and then multiplying by 100 to express it as a percentage.</p>
               <div class="math-equation">$$ \mathit{Ratio} = \frac{\mathit{Maintenance\,Cost}}{\mathit{Total\,Operating\,Cost}} \times \mathit{100} $$</div><ul><li><i>Maintenance Cost:</i> The numerator of the fraction.</li><li><i>Total Operating Cost:</i> The denominator of the fraction.</li></ul><script>if (window.MathJax) {  if (MathJax.typesetPromise) { MathJax.typesetPromise(); }  else if (MathJax.typeset) { MathJax.typeset(); }}</script>
            </td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Used in KPI(s)</td>
            <td><ul><li>This KPI is utilized in financial assessments and operational efficiency evaluations within the maintenance management framework</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Input Measure</td>
            <td><ul><li>The inputs for this KPI include the total maintenance costs incurred and the total operating costs for the period being analyzed</li></ul></td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Unit of Measure</td>
            <td>ratio (dimensionless)</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Reporting Source</td>
            <td>The data for this KPI is typically sourced from financial reports and maintenance logs that detail expenditures and operational expenses.</td>
         </tr>
         <tr>
            <td>Comments</td>
            <td>A higher ratio may indicate a greater reliance on maintenance, which could suggest the need for improved operational efficiency or equipment reliability.</td>
         </tr>
      </tbody>
   </table>

.. admonition:: Code Context
   :class: dropdown

   .. code-block:: python
      :linenos:
   
      :emphasize-lines: 5
   

      def calculate_maintenance_to_operating_cost_ratio(maintenance_cost: float, total_operating_cost: float) -> float:
       """Calculates the ratio of maintenance costs to total operating costs."""
       if total_operating_cost == 0:
           return 0.0
       # KPI: Maintenance to Operating Cost Ratio
       # Used for budgeting and assessing the overall cost impact of the maintenance strategy.
       ratio = (maintenance_cost / total_operating_cost) * 100
       return ratio
